coordinateBridge.fkPolicy: projected FK arm solution only; no IK, no roll correction, no production retarget mutation

findings: Relative to FPS-projected Weapon.R, current real attachment hilt differs by 0.46993 avg; this is a target-landmark mismatch, not automatically a bad Meshy hand grip.
Socket-relative saber basis is still wrong: current blade axis error is 46.659 deg avg and socket-relative tip error is 0.6199 avg.
Attachment-rotation-only experiment drops blade axis error to 0 deg and socket-relative tip error to 0.31254 avg, isolating basis/rotation as the dominant local weapon issue.
Tip-landmark-only experiment drops blade axis error to 0 deg but leaves FPS-projected tip error at 0.46993 avg because the socket target is still offset.
Position-only experiment proves socket placement alone is insufficient: blade axis stays 46.659 deg avg and tip error stays 0.6199 avg.
Scale-only experiment leaves blade axis error at 46.659 deg avg, so attachment scale is secondary.
